diagrams.title: Database Security Defense Layers
diagrams.description: A layered security model showing concentric rings of protection around the database, illustrating the Defense in Depth approach. Each layer represents a different security control that must be breached before reaching sensitive data.
elements: Outer ring: Network Security (firewalls, network segmentation, intrusion detection)
Second ring: Access Management (authentication, authorization, MFA)
Third ring: Data Protection (encryption at rest, encryption in transit, data masking)
Fourth ring: Database Security (SQL firewall, privileged access management, row-level security)
Inner ring: Monitoring & Auditing (audit logs, anomaly detection, alerting)
Center: Database Server with sensitive data icon
Arrows showing threats being blocked at each layer
diagrams.purpose: Explains the Defense in Depth concept, showing that database security requires multiple overlapping security controls rather than a single perimeter. Helps security professionals understand where different security tools fit in the overall architecture.

diagrams.title: Common Database Threats & Attack Vectors
diagrams.description: A visual map of the primary threats targeting databases, arranged by attack source (internal vs external) and attack type. Shows how different threats exploit different vulnerabilities.
elements: External Threats: SQL Injection attacks, DDoS attacks, Brute force attacks, Phishing campaigns, Malware/Ransomware
Internal Threats: Insider attacks, Privilege abuse, Accidental data exposure, Unpatched vulnerabilities
Application Layer Threats: API vulnerabilities, Weak authentication, Session hijacking
Connection arrows showing attack paths to the database
Severity indicators (high/medium/low) for each threat type
diagrams.purpose: Helps security teams understand the landscape of threats they need to defend against. Visualizing the various attack vectors emphasizes why multiple security controls are necessary and where monitoring should focus.

diagrams.title: Database Security Controls Framework
diagrams.description: A structured grid layout showing the key security controls organized by function: Preventive, Detective, and Corrective controls. Each category lists specific technologies and practices.
elements: Three-column layout with headers: Preventive Controls, Detective Controls, Corrective Controls
Preventive: Encryption, Access Control Lists, Firewalls, MFA, Data Masking, Network Segmentation, Input Validation
Corrective: Backup & Recovery, Patch Management, Incident Response Plans, Privilege Revocation
Detective: Audit Logging, Database Activity Monitoring, Intrusion Detection, Alert Systems, Security Information and Event Management (SIEM)
Icons for each control type (lock for encryption, eye for monitoring, shield for prevention)
Compliance standards badges at bottom (PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, SOC 2)
diagrams.purpose: Provides a comprehensive view of all security controls needed for database protection. Helps security architects ensure they've covered all aspects of a security program and aligns controls with compliance requirements.
